title: elytrigia caespitosa subsp. platyphylla
intro: Elytrigia caespitosa subsp. platyphylla, also known as wild ryegrass or prairie grass, is a perennial grass species native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with medicinal applications including wound healing, fever reduction, and relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Elytrigia caespitosa subsp. platyphylla can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for various forms of consumption and application in folk medicine.
